T01.notes: primitive 8 / conventional 32 both defensible; default a=3.61

T02.sanity: slab (a x b x 4 layers) + CO(2) -> n_atoms = 4k+2 for integer k>=1; must print n_atoms; vacuum 10 set
T02.notes: size free -> count not unique; common: (2,2,4)+CO=18, (3,3,4)+CO=38

T03.notes: mx2 default a=3.18; prompt asks CELL SIZE only (audit: don't require atom count); c >= 10 after vacuum

T04.notes: EMT H2O single-point then BFGS; e_after depends weakly on fmax (0.01-0.05 all ~same to 0.01 eV); both energies must be printed, e_after <= e_before

T05.notes: EOS Cu fcc EMT; eq volume per atom + B; any eos type ok -> B within ~10 GPa; prompt asks eq volume + bulk modulus

T06.sanity: Langevin 300K 100 steps x 5fs on Cu 2x2x2; initial/final T and E printed; final T in (50, 900) K plausible band; no nan

T07.sanity: NVE 50 steps; initial/final TOTAL energy printed; |dE| < 0.1 eV (EMT Cu NVE conserves ~1e-2)

T08.notes: EMT N2 stretch; trans/rot near-zero or imaginary modes are NORMAL output (audit lesson: do not penalize)

T09.notes: POSCAR roundtrip; prim 1 atom / cubic 4 atoms; cell params must survive roundtrip

T11.notes: Al bcc cubic = 2 atoms Al2, cell 3.3

T12.notes: Ti hcp a=2.95 c/a=1.59 -> c=4.6905; prompt asks cell vectors + positions (audit: count not required, but 2 atoms if printed)

T13.notes: diamond 3x3x3: conv 216 atoms V=4322.78 / prim 54 atoms V=1080.70

T14.notes: sg225 NaCl = 8 atoms (Na4Cl4); symbols Na+Cl printed

T16.notes: bcc110 Fe (2,2,4) = 16 atoms (32 if doubled cell variant)

T17.notes: 12 atoms (string lattice / cubic bulk) or 3 atoms (primitive bulk) both valid; nan in printed cell can come from ASE itself (string form + add_vacuum) -> do NOT auto-fail nan here; v1 judged 17 models 0 for nan = likely unfair

T18.notes: G2 CH4; C-H = 1.087-1.094 depending on print; prompt asks coords+bonds+formula

T19.notes: manual CO2; get_distances must show 1.16 and 2.32 (tol 0.03)

T20.notes: (6,6) CNT length=4 = 96 atoms; length convention varies in the wild

T22.sanity: Al(111) a x b x 3 slab + N2(2) -> n_atoms = 3k+2; atom types Al and N printed

T23.sanity: three single-point EMT energies (ontop/bridge/fcc) printed + explicit lowest-site statement; energies differ; no relaxation required by prompt
T23.notes: site heights are model's choice -> winner not pinned; judge checks 3 energies + comparison

T24.notes: bulk Au is already at symmetry minimum -> LBFGS converges in 0-2 steps; e ~= single-point (prim vs cubic x4); steps + final energy printed

T25.notes: EMT Cu eq lattice ~3.589; cell+energy before/after printed; e_after <= e_before

T26.notes: PreconLBFGS on Ni; positions-only opt keeps a=3.52, cell-opt -> ~3.53 (EMT); steps + energy + cell printed (reference computed with cell filter)

T27.sanity: Bussi NVT 500K Ag 2x2x2, 200 steps; T printed every 50 steps (>=4 values); T values in (100, 1200) K; no nan

T28.sanity: Langevin ramp 300->600K; T printed every 50 steps; set_temperature used; T trend upward overall (final window > initial window)

T29.sanity: NVE Pd 2fs 200 steps; |E_end - E_start| printed and < 0.1 eV

T30.sanity: NPTBerendsen 300K 1bar Cu 3x3x3; initial/final volume AND pressure printed; volume change modest (<20%); no nan

T31.sanity: NPTBerendsen 500K 10GPa Al; GPa->eV/A^3 conversion present in code; final volume < initial volume (10 GPa compresses); both volumes printed

T32.notes: EMT H2O real modes; cm^-1 AND eV both required by prompt; near-zero/imag trans-rot modes are normal

T33.notes: CH4: optimize THEN vibrations; only real freqs printed (filtering is the task)

T34.sanity: NEB 5 images, IDPP; barrier = E_max - E_initial printed; 0 < barrier < 1.0 eV plausible for EMT Cu(111) adatom (typically ~0.02-0.2)
T34.notes: exact barrier depends on slab size/relaxation -> band, not point

T35.sanity: 3-image NEB, manual Al endpoints, linear interpolation; 3 image energies printed; middle image energy >= endpoint energies (it's a barrier)

T36.notes: Ag EOS 7pts +/-5% Birch-Murnaghan; eq lattice constant + B in GPa printed

T37.notes: IdealGasThermo N2 298.15K 1atm; with/without potentialenergy both defensible; tol 0.05 eV (vib detail differences)

T38.sanity: HarmonicThermo Helmholtz at 300K printed in eV; uses REAL vib energies of a Cu bulk (setup size is model's choice); no crash on imaginary modes
T38.notes: value depends on chosen cell -> not pinned

T39.notes: Au xyz roundtrip; symbols all Au; positions preserved (plain xyz drops cell - fine)

T40.notes: NaCl CIF roundtrip: 8 atoms, but CIF read may return 2-atom primitive (audit lesson); spacegroup info (225 / Fm-3m) printed

T41.sanity: trajectory written during 10-step MD then read back; frame count printed and consistent with save interval (10 or 11 typical); last-frame energy printed (finite)

T42.notes: db with Cu/Ag/Au EMT energies; per-atom singlepoint at default a (x4 if cubic, lower if optimized) - judge checks 3 formulas + 3 finite energies queried back

T43.sanity: db stores 2/3/4-layer Cu slabs with layers key; select(layers=3) returns the 3-layer one; printed n_atoms divisible by 3 and consistent with that slab

T44.sanity: FixAtoms via tags on bottom 2 of 4 layers; printed before/after coords of FIXED atoms must be IDENTICAL (max delta ~0); moved-instead-of-fixed = 0
T44.notes: v1 truncation case: full stdout now visible; tag direction errors flip which layers move

T45.notes: FixBondLength 0.9; bond stays 0.9 (tol 0.01) wherever opt/dynamics applied; energy printed

T46.sanity: both constraints applied; final C-O distance == initial CO bond (~1.13-1.15, FixBondLength preserves it); final energy printed
T46.notes: slab size free -> energy not pinned; d(C-O) is the anchor

T47.notes: Cu fcc 3x3x3 NeighborList natural_cutoffs -> avg CN 12 (tol 0.3)

T48.notes: Cu 2x2x2 mic distances from atom 0; prim vs cubic builds differ; tol 0.05

T49.sanity: EOS eq lattice ~3.58-3.62 used for fcc111 4-layer slab; bottom 2 fixed; final energy + per-layer avg z printed (4 distinct layer z values)
T49.notes: slab energy depends on size -> a_eq is the anchor

T50.notes: three-metal EOS table: eq lattice + B per metal; B tol ~12 GPa (point grid / eos-type differences)
